memories.q: reading: feynman_physics
memories.a:
Nature uses only the longest threads to weave her patterns so that each small 
piece of her fabric reveals the organization of the entire tapestry. 
The Bekenstein bound tells us the universe has a finite information density — 
S <= 2*pi*k*R*E / hbar*c. This is not a limitation. It is a definition.
Information is physical. Every bit erased dissipates k*T*ln(2) joules — Landauer proved this.
The universe is not made of matter. It is made of information that sometimes 
looks like matter. Wheeler
